enrichment.short_description: Mollie is a European payment processor offering online and in-person payment acceptance, subscriptions, invoicing, and business accounts.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Mollie: pay-per-transaction payment processing for online and in-person payments, serving 250,000+ businesses across Europe.

features.title: Online payments
features.description: Accepts cards, PayPal, SEPA, iDEAL, Klarna, Apple Pay and Google Pay with no monthly minimums or contracts.

features.title: In-person payments
features.description: Point-of-sale card terminals with pay-as-you-go or contracted Pro pricing.

features.title: Recurring payments
features.description: Built-in subscription and recurring billing support.

features.title: Payment links
features.description: Generate shareable payment links without building a full checkout.

features.title: Dashboard & reconciliation
features.description: Centralized dashboard for payment management, insights, and accounting.

features.title: Fraud and risk management
features.description: Built-in fraud acceptance and risk controls on transactions.

pros: No monthly minimums, contracts, or hidden fees on standard pricing
Wide range of European-focused payment methods (iDEAL, SEPA, Klarna, Wero)
Serves over 250,000 businesses from startups to enterprises
Prebuilt e-commerce integrations and libraries for JS, PHP, .NET, and Python
cons: Per-transaction fees vary widely by payment method and region, making total cost harder to predict
Non-EEA card transactions carry a higher rate (3.25% + €0.25)
In-person Pro plan requires a 1-year contract

faqs.question: Does Mollie charge a monthly fee?
faqs.answer: Standard online payment pricing has no monthly fee — you only pay per successful transaction.

faqs.question: What payment methods does Mollie support?
faqs.answer: Cards, PayPal, SEPA Direct Debit and Bank Transfer, iDEAL, Klarna, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and more, with fees varying by method.

faqs.question: Does Mollie support in-person payments?
faqs.answer: Yes, via a Pay as You Go plan (€0/month) or a Pro plan (€20/month with a 1-year contract) using card terminals.

faqs.question: Is there volume pricing for high-volume merchants?
faqs.answer: Businesses processing €100,000+ per month can get tailored volume pricing, and startups may have fees waived up to €1,000,000 depending on funding.

use_cases.title: Online checkout for e-commerce
use_cases.description: Accept a wide range of European and global payment methods on a website or app.

use_cases.title: In-store card payments
use_cases.description: Use Mollie's terminals for point-of-sale payments alongside online sales.

use_cases.title: Subscription billing
use_cases.description: Charge customers on a recurring basis using Mollie's recurring payments API.

features: Rates vary by payment method and region

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Mollie?
paragraphs: Mollie is a payment processing platform built for European businesses, covering online payments, in-person card terminals, subscriptions, invoicing, and reconciliation.

It uses pay-per-transaction pricing with no fixed monthly cost for its standard online payments plan, and supports region-specific methods like iDEAL, SEPA, and Klarna alongside cards and digital wallets.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Mollie is for
paragraphs: Mollie suits startups, SaaS platforms, marketplaces, and enterprises operating primarily in Europe that need flexible payment methods without long-term contracts, plus optional in-person payment hardware.

enrichment.short_description: Checkout.com is a global enterprise payment processor for online payment acceptance, money movement, fraud protection, and payment optimization.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Checkout.com processes global payments for enterprises with local acquiring in 50+ countries, fraud protection, and custom flat-rate or interchange++ pricing.

features.title: Connect
features.description: Online payment acceptance across major card schemes and local payment methods.

features.title: Move
features.description: Global money movement and payouts to entities and customers.

features.title: Boost
features.description: AI-powered payment optimization through Intelligent Acceptance.

features.title: Protect
features.description: Fraud detection, authentication, and dispute management.

features.title: Manage
features.description: Dashboard and financial controls for reconciliation and reporting.

pros: Local acquiring capabilities in over 50 countries
Processed $300BN in ecommerce payments in 2025
Supports 150+ processing currencies
Free processing for registered charities
cons: No public pricing — requires contacting sales for a custom quote
Enterprise-oriented, likely less suited to very small merchants
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site

faqs.question: How much does Checkout.com cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is tailored per business via flat-rate or interchange++ fee models — there is no published price list; you contact their sales team for a quote.

faqs.question: Does Checkout.com support multiple currencies?
faqs.answer: Yes, it facilitates transactions in more than 150 processing currencies.

faqs.question: Is Checkout.com free for charities?
faqs.answer: Yes, registered charities in supported countries can process payments at no cost.

faqs.question: Who uses Checkout.com?
faqs.answer: Enterprise merchants in ecommerce, gaming, marketplaces, travel, fintech, and crypto, including Microsoft, Spotify, eBay, Uber, and Klarna.

use_cases.title: Global online checkout
use_cases.description: Accept major card schemes and local payment methods across 50+ countries with local acquiring.

use_cases.title: Payout and disbursement
use_cases.description: Move funds globally to business entities and end customers.

use_cases.title: Fraud and dispute management
use_cases.description: Detect fraud, authenticate transactions, and manage chargebacks/disputes.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Checkout.com?
paragraphs: Checkout.com is a global payment services provider that lets businesses accept online payments and disburse funds through local acquiring in over 50 countries and 150+ currencies.
Its platform bundles payment acceptance, global money movement, AI-based acceptance optimization, fraud/dispute protection, and a management dashboard.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Checkout.com is for
paragraphs: Checkout.com targets enterprise merchants across ecommerce, gaming, marketplaces, travel, fintech, and crypto that need global acquiring and custom-negotiated pricing rather than a self-serve small business plan.

enrichment.short_description: Braintree, a PayPal company, is an enterprise payment processing platform for cards, PayPal, Venmo, and digital wallets with fraud protection.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Braintree (PayPal) processes cards, PayPal, Venmo, and digital wallets with standard rates of 2.89% + $0.29 per transaction and no monthly fees.

features.title: Unified checkout
features.description: Single-touch payments across cards, PayPal, Venmo, and digital wallets.

features.title: Mobile SDKs
features.description: SDKs for integrating payments into mobile apps.

features.title: Global currency acceptance
features.description: Accepts payments and issues payouts to 200+ markets in 50+ currencies.

features.title: Fraud and risk management
features.description: Adaptive fraud protection and PCI DSS Level 1 compliant processing.

features.title: Payment orchestration
features.description: Routes and manages payments across methods from one integration.

features.title: Sandbox environment
features.description: Free developer sandbox for testing integrations before going live.

pros: No monthly fees, setup fees, or minimum transaction fees on standard pricing
Backed by PayPal's global payments infrastructure
Supports cards, PayPal, Venmo, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and local payment methods
Discounted charity rate available (2.19% + $0.29)
cons: Standard rate (2.89% + $0.29) is higher than some flat-rate competitors
Custom/negotiated pricing for high-volume merchants ($80,000+/month) requires contacting sales
Additional fees apply for non-USD currency and cards issued outside the US

faqs.question: What are Braintree's standard transaction fees?
faqs.answer: 2.89% + $0.29 for cards and digital wallets, 3.49% + $0.49 for Venmo, and 0.75% (capped at $5) for standard ACH Direct Debit.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Braintree offers a free sandbox environment for testing with no obligation to apply for an account.

faqs.question: Does Braintree charge monthly fees?
faqs.answer: No monthly fees are listed for standard commercial pricing.

faqs.question: Does Braintree offer nonprofit pricing?
faqs.answer: Yes, verified 501(c)(3) organizations get a reduced rate of 2.19% + $0.29 on cards and digital wallets.

use_cases.title: Unified online checkout
use_cases.description: Accept cards, PayPal, Venmo, and wallets through one integration.

use_cases.title: Mobile app payments
use_cases.description: Embed payments in iOS/Android apps via Braintree's mobile SDKs.

use_cases.title: Global payouts
use_cases.description: Send payouts to recipients across 200+ markets and 50+ currencies.

features: Flat-rate or interchange-plus pricing for $80,000+/month volume

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Braintree?
paragraphs: Braintree is PayPal's enterprise payment processing platform, offering end-to-end checkout across cards, PayPal, Venmo, and digital wallets with a single integration.
It includes mobile SDKs, adaptive fraud protection, PCI DSS Level 1 compliance, and payouts to over 200 markets in 50+ currencies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Braintree is for
paragraphs: Braintree is aimed at enterprise businesses that want a single payments integration covering cards, PayPal, and Venmo, with custom pricing available for higher-volume processors.

enrichment.short_description: FastSpring is a merchant-of-record platform for selling software, SaaS, and digital products globally with automated tax compliance.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: FastSpring is a merchant of record handling global payments, tax compliance, and subscription billing for software and digital product sellers.

features.title: Global online payments
features.description: Branded, localized checkout supporting 200+ regions and 35+ currencies.

features.title: Automated tax compliance
features.description: Handles VAT and sales tax as the merchant of record.

features.title: Subscription management
features.description: Manages recurring billing for SaaS and subscription products.

features.title: Fraud prevention
features.description: Built-in risk management and fraud prevention on transactions.

features.title: Affiliate marketing network
features.description: Built-in affiliate program tools for driving referral sales.

features.title: Digital invoicing and quotes
features.description: Interactive quotes and invoicing for B2B sales.

features.title: Developer APIs
features.description: REST APIs, webhooks, and JavaScript libraries with documentation and code samples.

pros: Acts as merchant of record, taking on tax and compliance liability
Supports 21+ languages and 35+ currencies across 200+ regions
Serves 3,200+ customers and processes over $2B in transactions per year
All features included in flat-rate pricing rather than charged à la carte
cons: No public pricing list — rates are negotiated per business with an account executive
Custom flat-rate pricing makes upfront cost comparison harder

faqs.question: How much does FastSpring cost?
faqs.answer: FastSpring uses simple, flat-rate pricing based on transaction type and business volume, determined by talking with their team — there is no fixed public price list.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: FastSpring's site offers a 'Try Now For Free' option, though specific trial terms aren't detailed on the homepage.

faqs.question: What does FastSpring handle as a merchant of record?
faqs.answer: It manages global payments, tax compliance, fraud prevention, and reporting on behalf of sellers.

faqs.question: When was FastSpring founded and where is it based?
faqs.answer: FastSpring was founded in 2006 and is headquartered in Santa Barbara, California, with additional offices in England, the Netherlands, and Canada.

use_cases.title: Global SaaS sales
use_cases.description: Sell subscriptions internationally with localized checkout and automated tax handling.

use_cases.title: Digital product and game sales
use_cases.description: Process one-time and recurring purchases for software, games, and digital courses.

use_cases.title: Affiliate-driven sales
use_cases.description: Use the built-in affiliate network to drive additional revenue.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FastSpring?
paragraphs: FastSpring, founded in 2006 and headquartered in Santa Barbara, California, is a merchant-of-record platform that lets businesses sell software, SaaS, and digital products globally.
It handles payment processing, subscription billing, automated tax compliance, fraud prevention, and affiliate marketing, supporting 200+ regions, 35+ currencies, and 21+ languages.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FastSpring is for
paragraphs: FastSpring suits software, SaaS, and digital product companies that want to offload global tax compliance and payment operations to a merchant of record rather than building this infrastructure themselves.

enrichment.short_description: Lemon Squeezy is a merchant-of-record platform, now owned by Stripe, handling payments, tax compliance, and subscriptions for software sellers.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Lemon Squeezy is a merchant of record for software sellers, charging 5% + $0.50 per transaction with no monthly fee; now owned by Stripe.

features.title: Merchant of record
features.description: Takes on tax collection, calculation, and compliance liability across jurisdictions.

features.title: Global payment acceptance
features.description: Accepts payments from 135+ countries and 130+ currencies with up to 21 payment methods, including PayPal.

features.title: Fraud protection
features.description: AI-based fraud system to assess risk and reduce chargebacks.

features.title: Dunning management
features.description: Handles failed card retries and customer emails to reduce involuntary subscription churn.

features.title: Reconciled revenue dashboard
features.description: Single dashboard for viewing reconciled revenue across sales.

features.title: No setup cost
features.description: Free to start with no trial requirement or contract.

pros: Single transaction fee (5% + $0.50) with no monthly fee
Acts as merchant of record, absorbing tax and compliance liability
Supports 135+ countries, 130+ currencies, and up to 21 payment methods
No setup cost or contract required to start
cons: Additional fees apply for international transactions (+1.5%), PayPal (+1.5%), subscriptions (+0.5%), abandoned cart recovery (+5%), and affiliates (+3%)
Now owned by Stripe and being evolved toward Stripe Managed Payments, which introduces migration uncertainty for existing merchants
Percentage-based pricing can be more costly at higher sales volumes than flat-rate processors

faqs.question: How much does Lemon Squeezy cost?
faqs.answer: Lemon Squeezy charges 5% + $0.50 per transaction with no monthly fee; additional fees apply for international payments, PayPal, subscriptions, abandoned cart recovery, and affiliates.

faqs.question: Is Lemon Squeezy still owned independently?
faqs.answer: No, Stripe acquired Lemon Squeezy in July 2024; the platform continues to operate under its own name while Stripe develops Stripe Managed Payments.

faqs.question: What does Lemon Squeezy handle as a merchant of record?
faqs.answer: It manages tax collection and calculation, compliance, fraud protection, and dispute handling on behalf of sellers.

faqs.question: How many payment methods does Lemon Squeezy support?
faqs.answer: Up to 21 payment methods, including PayPal, across 135+ countries.

use_cases.title: Selling digital products and SaaS
use_cases.description: Handle global payments and tax compliance for software or digital product sales without building the infrastructure in-house.

use_cases.title: Subscription billing
use_cases.description: Manage recurring subscription payments with built-in dunning to reduce churn.

features: +1.5% international, +1.5% PayPal, +0.5% subscriptions, +5% abandoned cart recovery, +3% affiliates

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Lemon Squeezy?
paragraphs: Lemon Squeezy is a merchant-of-record platform for selling software, SaaS, and digital products, handling global payments, tax compliance, and fraud protection under a single 5% + $0.50 transaction fee.

Stripe acquired Lemon Squeezy in July 2024. As of 2026, Lemon Squeezy continues to operate under its own name while Stripe builds out Stripe Managed Payments, a related merchant-of-record product, with existing Lemon Squeezy merchants able to migrate over time.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Lemon Squeezy is for
paragraphs: Lemon Squeezy suits indie developers, SaaS founders, and small software businesses that want to sell globally without setting up their own tax compliance and payment infrastructure.

enrichment.short_description: Orb is a revenue design platform that automates usage-based billing, pricing strategy, and invoicing for SaaS and AI companies.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Orb automates usage-based billing, pricing changes, and invoicing for SaaS and AI companies, with custom pricing based on billings and events.

features.title: Billing engine
features.description: Automates usage-based and hybrid pricing models.

features.title: Usage metering
features.description: Real-time event ingestion and usage tracking for billing.

features.title: Price modeling
features.description: Tools for designing, evolving, and simulating pricing changes before rollout.

features.title: Orb Invoicing
features.description: Automated invoicing tied to metered usage and contracts.

features.title: Spend controls
features.description: Alerts and limits to help customers manage usage-based spend.

features.title: Experience Kit
features.description: Prebuilt dashboards and checkout flow components.

features.title: Finance integrations
features.description: Connects usage data to finance workflows, including Salesforce and NetSuite on higher tiers.

pros: Purpose-built for usage-based and hybrid pricing models
Includes pricing simulation tools to test changes before rollout
Used by notable companies including Vercel, Supabase, and Replit
Higher tiers add Salesforce, NetSuite, and data warehouse sync
cons: No public pricing — all tiers (Core, Advanced, Enterprise) require contacting sales
Pricing depends on billings and events plus a platform fee, making costs hard to estimate upfront
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site

faqs.question: How is Orb priced?
faqs.answer: Orb bases pricing on two metrics — billings and events — plus a platform fee on the Advanced and Enterprise tiers; exact pricing requires contacting sales.

faqs.question: What plans does Orb offer?
faqs.answer: Core, Advanced (most popular), and Enterprise, each requiring a custom quote.

faqs.question: Does Orb integrate with CRM and finance tools?
faqs.answer: The Advanced tier adds Salesforce and NetSuite integrations plus data warehouse sync.

faqs.question: Who uses Orb?
faqs.answer: SaaS, AI, and infrastructure companies such as Vercel, Supabase, Redis, LaunchDarkly, and Replit.

use_cases.title: Usage-based billing
use_cases.description: Meter and bill customers based on consumption of a product or API.

use_cases.title: Pricing experimentation
use_cases.description: Simulate and roll out pricing changes to customer cohorts before full launch.

use_cases.title: AI product monetization
use_cases.description: Bill for usage of AI or agentic products based on consumption metrics.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Orb?
paragraphs: Orb is a revenue design platform that automates usage-based billing, pricing modeling, and invoicing for growing software companies.
It offers three tiers — Core, Advanced, and Enterprise — all priced on a custom basis tied to billings and event volume, with higher tiers adding CRM and finance integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Orb is for
paragraphs: Orb is aimed at SaaS, AI, and infrastructure companies that need flexible usage-based or hybrid pricing models rather than simple flat subscription billing.

enrichment.short_description: Metronome, now part of Stripe, is a usage-based billing platform for metering, invoicing, and monetizing SaaS and AI products.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Metronome is a usage-based billing platform, now part of Stripe, offering real-time metering, invoicing, and spend controls for SaaS and AI companies.

features.title: Real-time metering
features.description: Meters usage events in real time for billing.

features.title: Rate cards
features.description: Customizable pricing rate cards that can be updated quickly.

features.title: In-product billing dashboards
features.description: Embeddable dashboards giving customers visibility into their usage and spend.

features.title: Spend controls
features.description: Configurable alerts and limits on customer spend.

features.title: Usage and revenue analytics
features.description: Real-time reporting on usage and revenue.

features.title: Pricing experimentation
features.description: Cohort-based rollouts for testing new pricing models.

features.title: Stripe integration
features.description: Native integration with Stripe for payment processing.

pros: Starter plan includes $100,000 in billing volume and 10M events before overage fees
Supports usage-based, seat-based, subscription, and hybrid pricing models
Native Stripe integration since Metronome is now part of Stripe
Used by companies including OpenAI, Cribl, and Fly.io
cons: Custom plan pricing is not publicly listed and requires contacting sales
Overage pricing (0.8% of billing volume, $0.04/1k events) adds cost unpredictability at scale
No free trial or free plan mentioned on the site

faqs.question: How is Metronome priced?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan includes $100,000 in billing volume (0.8% after) and 10M events ($0.04 per 1,000 events after); the Custom plan is quote-based.

faqs.question: Is Metronome still an independent company?
faqs.answer: No, Metronome is now part of Stripe, combining both companies' monetization capabilities.

faqs.question: What pricing models does Metronome support?
faqs.answer: Usage-based, seat-based, subscription, and hybrid pricing models.

faqs.question: Does Metronome integrate with Stripe?
faqs.answer: Yes, it has a native Stripe integration for payment processing.

use_cases.title: Usage-based SaaS billing
use_cases.description: Meter product usage and generate invoices based on consumption.

use_cases.title: AI product monetization
use_cases.description: Bill for consumption of AI or agentic products with real-time metering.

use_cases.title: Pricing experimentation
use_cases.description: Test new pricing models on customer cohorts before a full rollout.

pricing_plans.price: $100,000 billing volume included, 0.8% after; 10M events included, $0.04/1k events after
features: Usage-based, seat-based, subscription, and hybrid pricing

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Metronome?
paragraphs: Metronome is a usage-based billing platform providing monetization infrastructure for SaaS companies, including real-time metering, rate cards, spend controls, and revenue analytics.
Metronome is now part of Stripe. Its Starter plan includes $100,000 in billing volume and 10M events before usage-based overage fees apply; larger customers use a custom quote-based plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Metronome is for
paragraphs: Metronome is built for fast-growing SaaS, AI, and infrastructure companies — including customers like OpenAI, Cribl, and Fly.io — that need consumption-based billing rather than flat subscriptions.

enrichment.short_description: Billsby is subscription billing software for managing recurring payments, dunning, tax compliance, and revenue reporting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Billsby is subscription billing software with a free testing tier and paid plans from $45/month plus a small percentage on revenue over $15,000.

features.title: Products, plans and cycles
features.description: Set up unlimited subscription products, plans, and billing cycles.

features.title: Multiple payment gateways
features.description: Supports multiple payment gateways on higher plans, including ACH processing.

features.title: Integrated checkout
features.description: Embeddable checkout implementable with minimal code.

features.title: Automated dunning
features.description: Automated dunning management to recover failed payments.

features.title: Tax compliance
features.description: Built-in tax compliance functionality across multiple regions on the Pro plan.

features.title: Advanced reporting
features.description: Reporting and analytics on subscription revenue.

features.title: Developer API
features.description: Full API access on the Pro plan for custom integrations.

pros: Free plan allows unlimited testing and setup before going live
Core plan starts at a flat $45/month with a low percentage fee on revenue over $15,000
Pro plan adds multi-language support, ACH processing, and full API access
Rated 4.8 stars and described as the '#1 Rated Subscription Billing Software' on G2
cons: Higher-value features like multiple payment gateways and ACH are limited to the Pro plan
Custom/enterprise pricing is quote-only
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Billsby have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan allows unlimited testing and setup before going live, with no time limit.

faqs.question: How much does Billsby's Core plan cost?
faqs.answer: $45/month for up to 5 users, plus 0.4% on revenue exceeding $15,000/month.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan add?
faqs.answer: User permissions, multiple payment gateways, ACH processing, multiple tax regions, FreeAgent/TaxJar integrations, advanced reporting, multi-language support, and full API access, for $135/month plus 0.5% on revenue over $30,000/month.

faqs.question: Is custom pricing available?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Custom plan with unlimited users is available via contacting the sales team.

use_cases.title: Subscription setup and testing
use_cases.description: Build and test subscription products and pricing on the free plan before launch.

use_cases.title: Recurring revenue management
use_cases.description: Manage plans, dunning, and reporting for an established subscription business.

use_cases.title: Multi-region billing
use_cases.description: Handle multiple tax regions and payment gateways on the Pro plan.

features: Full setup access before going live

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Billsby?
paragraphs: Billsby is subscription billing software that helps businesses manage products, plans, billing cycles, dunning, tax compliance, and reporting for recurring revenue.
It offers a free plan for setup and testing, a Core plan at $45/month, a Pro plan at $135/month with more integrations and multi-language support, and a custom Enterprise tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Billsby is for
paragraphs: Billsby suits startups and small-to-mid-size businesses building subscription products that want to start free while testing and scale into paid plans as revenue grows.

enrichment.short_description: Pendo is an AI-powered product analytics and experience platform for tracking user behavior, in-app guides, and feedback.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pendo offers a free plan up to 500 MAUs plus custom-priced Base, Core, and Ultimate plans for product analytics, in-app guides, and session replay.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Captures and analyzes user behavior across software products.

features.title: Agent Analytics
features.description: Measures adoption and impact of AI agents within a product.

features.title: In-app guides
features.description: Contextual in-app guidance to drive feature adoption.

features.title: Session Replay
features.description: Replays real user sessions to see experiences firsthand (Core plan and above).

features.title: Listen
features.description: Converts user feedback into structured insights.

features.title: Predict
features.description: Identifies churn risk before it happens.

features.title: Leo
features.description: Natural-language question answering over product data.

features.title: Data Sync
features.description: Integrates product usage data across other systems.

pros: Free plan available for up to 500 monthly active users with no credit card required
Used by 14,000+ teams and tracks over 1 billion users across 35 trillion collected events
Integrates with 50,000+ agents and apps, including Claude and ChatGPT
Novus (beta) offers continuous product monitoring and AI-generated PRs at no cost during beta
cons: Base, Core, and Ultimate plans require contacting sales for custom pricing rather than published rates
Advanced features like session replay and sentiment surveys are gated behind higher tiers
Founding year, headquarters, and company size not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Pendo have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, a free plan supports up to 500 monthly active users with product analytics, in-app guides, and Pendo-branded roadmaps/NPS surveys, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How is Pendo priced beyond the free plan?
faqs.answer: Base, Core, and Ultimate plans use custom pricing based on monthly active users (MAUs); contact sales for a quote.

faqs.question: What is Pendo's Agent Analytics?
faqs.answer: An add-on that measures the adoption and impact of AI agents within a product, priced on prompt volume with a free allowance included.

faqs.question: Does Pendo integrate with AI assistants?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pendo's integrations include Claude and ChatGPT among its 50,000+ supported agents and apps.

use_cases.title: Product usage analytics
use_cases.description: Track how users interact with a product to identify friction points.

use_cases.title: In-app onboarding and guidance
use_cases.description: Use in-app guides to drive feature adoption and reduce churn.

use_cases.title: AI agent monitoring
use_cases.description: Measure adoption and effectiveness of AI agents embedded in a product.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pendo?
paragraphs: Pendo is a product analytics and experience platform that captures user behavior across software and AI agents, surfaces where users struggle, and provides in-app guidance to improve adoption.
It offers a free plan for up to 500 MAUs, plus Base, Core, and Ultimate tiers priced on custom MAU volume, alongside add-ons like Predict for churn risk and Agent Analytics for AI adoption tracking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pendo is for
paragraphs: Pendo serves product, sales, IT, and enterprise teams that need to understand user behavior and drive adoption, spanning startups to large enterprises across 14,000+ teams.

enrichment.short_description: Userpilot is a product analytics and onboarding platform for in-app engagement, user feedback, and retention.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Userpilot is a product analytics and onboarding platform starting at $299/month with in-app engagement, surveys, and session replay.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Event autocapture and custom dashboards on the Growth plan and above.

features.title: In-app engagement
features.description: Tooltips, banners, spotlights, and flows to guide users.

features.title: User feedback surveys
features.description: NPS and CSAT surveys to collect user feedback.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Session replay available as an add-on on the Growth plan.

features.title: Email and mobile engagement
features.description: Email engagement built in, with mobile engagement available as an add-on.

features.title: Lia AI agent
features.description: AI-powered agent for analyzing data and automating in-app actions.

features.title: Workflows
features.description: Multi-channel engagement workflows across in-app, email, and other channels.

pros: Free trial available with no credit card required
Starter plan includes SOC 2 Type 2, HIPAA, and GDPR compliance
Growth and Enterprise plans add advanced analytics, event autocapture, and premium integrations
Used by 1,200 companies across product, UX, customer success, marketing, and growth teams
cons: Starting price of $299/month is higher than some onboarding tool alternatives
Session replay and mobile engagement are paid add-ons rather than included features
Enterprise plan pricing is custom/quote-only
Founding year, headquarters, and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: How much does Userpilot cost?
faqs.answer: The Starter plan is $299/month for up to 2,000 MAUs; the Growth plan starts from $849/month; Enterprise pricing is custom.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Userpilot offers a free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What is Lia?
faqs.answer: Lia is Userpilot's AI-powered agent for analyzing product data and automating in-app actions.

faqs.question: What integrations does Userpilot support?
faqs.answer: Salesforce, HubSpot, Segment, Mixpanel, Zendesk, Intercom, Google Analytics, Amplitude, and webhooks/API connections.

use_cases.title: User onboarding
use_cases.description: Guide new users through in-app tooltips, flows, and spotlights.

use_cases.title: Product analytics
use_cases.description: Track feature usage and engagement trends with autocaptured events.

use_cases.title: Retention and feedback
use_cases.description: Collect NPS/CSAT feedback and re-engage users via email workflows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Userpilot?
paragraphs: Userpilot is a product analytics and onboarding platform combining in-app engagement (tooltips, flows, spotlights), product analytics, feedback surveys, and session replay.
Pricing starts at $299/month for up to 2,000 MAUs, scaling to a Growth plan from $849/month and a custom Enterprise tier, with a free trial available on signup.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Userpilot is for
paragraphs: Userpilot is used by product management, UX/design, customer success, marketing, and growth teams at around 1,200 companies to drive user activation and retention.
